title: GLBA §314.4(c)(1) — Access controls: customer-information access governance and least-privilege enforcement
exclusions: Does NOT substitute for the full regulatory control set; this UC provides one of several evidence streams and must be combined with policy, procedure, and supplemental UCs to demonstrate the complete clause.
evidence: Scheduled search output persisted to the compliance summary index with regulation=GLBA, clause=§314.4(c)(1), available as a saved search and exportable as a CSV evidence pack for the responsible officer.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Phase-C authored UC targeting GLBA §314.4(c)(1) (Access controls). Monitors access to customer information systems and enforces least-privilege; assurance level set to 'contributing' pending SME review per Phase E of the regulation-coverage plan.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that GLBA §314.4(c)(1) (Access controls) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.50.38: GLBA §314.4(c)(1) — Access controls: customer-information access governance and least-privilege enforcement.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.50.38' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.
compliance.smeCaveat: Phase-C draft: SME review required before uplifting assurance from 'contributing' to 'partial' or 'full'. Confirm that the SPL captures the specific regulator expectation and that the telemetry exists in the deployment.

dataSources: IAM access logs, privilege-escalation events, access-review campaign results, data-classification tags.
app: Splunk Enterprise Security, Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform
spl:
index=iam sourcetype IN (access_log, privilege_changes) earliest=-30d
| search data_classification="customer_information" OR system_tag="glba_scope"
| eval privileged_access=if(role IN ("admin", "dba", "root", "superuser"), 1, 0)
| eval access_reviewed=if(isnotnull(last_access_review) AND (now()-strptime(last_access_review, "%Y-%m-%d"))<15552000, 1, 0)
| stats count AS total_accesses sum(privileged_access) AS privileged_count dc(user) AS distinct_users sum(access_reviewed) AS reviewed BY system, department
| eval privileged_pct=round(100*privileged_count/total_accesses, 1)
| eval review_pct=round(100*reviewed/total_accesses, 1)
| where privileged_pct>10 OR review_pct<100
| table system, department, total_accesses, distinct_users, privileged_count, privileged_pct, review_pct
description: Monitors access to customer-information systems, tracks privileged-access ratios, and verifies access-review coverage per GLBA §314.4(c)(1) requirements for access controls.
value: §314.4(c)(1) requires implementing access controls to limit access to customer information to authorised users. Continuous monitoring of privileged access and review completeness enforces least-privilege.
implementation: (1) Tag systems holding customer information with system_tag=glba_scope; (2) ingest IAM access and privilege-change logs; (3) schedule daily; (4) escalate excessive privileged access.
visualization: Table of flagged records for investigation; single-value KPI of coverage / compliance percentage; time chart of trend over the last 90 days; drill-down per responsible owner.
knownFalsePositives: Tuning prerequisites: source field mappings must be in place before the UC will produce reliable metrics; early runs will commonly flag unmapped systems until onboarding is completed.
grandmaExplanation: GLBA §314.4(c)(1) requires controlling who can access customer financial data — so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security, Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: IAM access logs, privilege-escalation events, access-review campaign results, data-classification tags.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Tag systems holding customer information with system_tag=glba_scope; (2) ingest IAM access and privilege-change logs; (3) schedule daily; (4) escalate excessive privileged access.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=iam sourcetype IN (access_log, privilege_changes) earliest=-30d
| search data_classification="customer_information" OR system_tag="glba_scope"
| eval privileged_access=if(role IN ("admin", "dba", "root", "superuser"), 1, 0)
| eval access_reviewed=if(isnotnull(last_access_review) AND (now()-strptime(last_access_review, "%Y-%m-%d"))<15552000, 1, 0)
| stats count AS total_accesses sum(privileged_access) AS privileged_count dc(user) AS distinct_users sum(access_reviewed) AS reviewed BY system, department
| eval privileged_pct=round(100*privileged_count/total_accesses, 1)
| eval review_pct=round(100*reviewed/total_accesses, 1)
| where privileged_pct>10 OR review_pct<100
| table system, department, total_accesses, distinct_users, privileged_count, privileged_pct, review_pct
```

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table of flagged records for investigation; single-value KPI of coverage / compliance percentage; time chart of trend over the last 90 days; drill-down per responsible owner.
